title: Evolution of Database Applications
description: A chronological timeline showing the major eras and technologies in the development of database management systems.
elements: 1980s: Rise of Relational Model (e.g., Oracle, DB2, SQL)
2000s: NoSQL & Big Data Databases (e.g., MongoDB, Cassandra)
2010s-Present: Cloud & Distributed Databases (e.g., AWS Aurora, Google Spanner)
relationships: Evolved from one era to the next, driven by increasing data volume, complexity, and need for scalability and flexibility.
